tests.finding: Explicit correct port works as expected
Wrong target port causes timeout (HTTP 000). Revision stuck in Activating. StartUp probe fails repeatedly (190+ times). Container running and healthy internally but ingress cannot reach it.

tests.deployment_method: az containerapp ingress update (fix targetPort from 80 to 8080)
tests.finding: Fixing targetPort recovers the app. First request takes ~13.5s (new replica activation). Subsequent requests are fast (~60ms).

tests.deployment_method: az containerapp up --source (with EXPOSE 9999 in Dockerfile)
tests.finding: Auto-detection (targetPort=0) with EXPOSE 9999 works. Platform correctly routes to port 9999. API reports targetPort=0 (auto-detect mode). First request slow due to cold start.

results.body: upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers. retried and the latest reset reason: remote connection failure, transport failure reason: delayed connect error: Connection refused
upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers...Connection refused
upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers...Connection refused
upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers...Connection refused

tests.finding: Explicit wrong port gives 503 with 'Connection refused'. Fast failure (~250-500ms). Envoy proxy reports 'upstream connect error'. Note: this app had been running before port change, so revision was already active.

tests.deployment_method: az containerapp up --source (NO EXPOSE in Dockerfile)
tests.finding: MAJOR FINDING: Auto-detection works even WITHOUT EXPOSE directive. Platform detected port 9999 at runtime (likely by scanning listening ports). This means EXPOSE is NOT required for auto-detection.
Explicit correct port on no-EXPOSE image works as expected

summary.auto_detection_mechanism: Container Apps auto-detection (targetPort=0) works by scanning listening ports at runtime, NOT by reading EXPOSE from Dockerfile. Both T3 (with EXPOSE) and T5 (without EXPOSE) succeeded equally.

wrong_port_failure_modes.fresh_deployment: Timeout (HTTP 000) + revision stuck in Activating + 190+ StartUp probe failures

summary.recovery: Fixing targetPort via ingress update recovers the app within ~13.5s (new replica spin-up)
key_insights: EXPOSE directive is NOT required for auto-detection
Auto-detection (targetPort=0) only available via 'az containerapp up', not 'az containerapp create'
Wrong port on fresh deployment = timeout + Activating stuck
Wrong port on running revision = 503 Connection refused
Container logs show app is healthy even when port mismatch exists
StartUp probe uses the configured targetPort, so wrong port = infinite probe failures
Recovery from wrong port is fast after correction (~13.5s)
